```python
# The periodic input |+0-> should concentrate measurements on frequency peaks
# after QFT, specifically on outcomes '000' and '100' which correspond to
# the frequency components of the periodic state
peak_outcomes = ['000', '100']
peak_counts = sum(counts.get(outcome, 0) for outcome in peak_outcomes)
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
peak_ratio = peak_counts / total_counts

# Assert that at least 80% of measurements fall on the expected frequency peaks
assert peak_ratio >= 0.8, f"Expected frequency peaks should contain ≥80% of counts, got {peak_ratio:.3f}"

# Assert that each individual peak has significant probability
for outcome in peak_outcomes:
    outcome_ratio = counts.get(outcome, 0) / total_counts
    assert outcome_ratio >= 0.15, f"Peak outcome {outcome} should have ≥15% probability, got {outcome_ratio:.3f}"
```

```python
# The QFT of the periodic input |+0-> should concentrate on specific frequency peaks
# Expected high-probability outcomes are '000' and '100' (frequency components)
expected_outcomes = {'000', '100'}
total_expected_prob = sum(counts.get(outcome, 0) for outcome in expected_outcomes)

# These outcomes should capture most of the probability mass
assert total_expected_prob > 0.8, f"Expected outcomes {expected_outcomes} should have >80% probability, got {total_expected_prob:.3f}"

# Individual expected outcomes should have reasonable probability
for outcome in expected_outcomes:
    prob = counts.get(outcome, 0)
    assert prob > 0.1, f"Expected outcome {outcome} should have >10% probability, got {prob:.3f}"

# No single unexpected outcome should dominate
unexpected_outcomes = {outcome for outcome in counts if outcome not in expected_outcomes}
for outcome in unexpected_outcomes:
    prob = counts.get(outcome, 0)
    assert prob < 0.3, f"Unexpected outcome {outcome} should have <30% probability, got {prob:.3f}"
```

```python
# The QFT of the periodic input state |+0-> should concentrate on specific frequency peaks
# Expected peaks are at computational basis states corresponding to the frequency components
total_measured = sum(counts.values())
peak_states = ['000', '100']  # Expected frequency peaks from QFT of |+0->

# Calculate probability mass on expected peaks
peak_probability = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in peak_states) / total_measured

# Verify that most of the probability mass is concentrated on the expected peaks
assert peak_probability > 0.8, f"Expected peaks concentration {peak_probability:.3f} too low"

# Verify that no individual unexpected state has excessive probability
max_other_probability = 0
for state in counts:
    if state not in peak_states:
        prob = counts[state] / total_measured
        max_other_probability = max(max_other_probability, prob)

assert max_other_probability < 0.3, f"Unexpected state probability {max_other_probability:.3f} too high"
```

```python
# The QFT should concentrate measurement outcomes on specific frequency peaks
# For a 3-qubit periodic input |+0-> = (|000> + |010> - |100> - |110>)/2,
# the QFT should produce peaks at specific frequencies

total_measured = sum(counts.values())
peak_outcomes = ['000', '001', '100', '101']  # Expected frequency domain peaks
peak_counts = sum(counts.get(outcome, 0) for outcome in peak_outcomes)
peak_probability = peak_counts / total_measured

# Assert that at least 70% of measurements fall on the expected frequency peaks
assert peak_probability >= 0.7, f"Peak concentration too low: {peak_probability:.3f} < 0.7"

# Assert that no single non-peak outcome dominates
non_peak_outcomes = ['010', '011', '110', '111']
for outcome in non_peak_outcomes:
    outcome_prob = counts.get(outcome, 0) / total_measured
    assert outcome_prob <= 0.2, f"Non-peak outcome {outcome} too prominent: {outcome_prob:.3f} > 0.2"
```

```python
# The QFT of the periodic input state |+0-> should concentrate on specific frequency peaks
# Expected peaks are at binary states 000 and 100 (decimal 0 and 4)
expected_peaks = {'000', '100'}

# Calculate total probability mass on expected peaks
peak_probability = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in expected_peaks)

# At least 80% of measurements should fall on the expected frequency peaks
assert peak_probability >= 0.8, f"Expected peaks {expected_peaks} should contain >=80% probability, got {peak_probability:.3f}"

# Verify we have reasonable statistics
assert sum(counts.values()) > 0, "No measurement counts recorded"

# Check that the dominant outcomes are indeed the expected peaks
sorted_outcomes = sorted(counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
top_two_states = {state for state, _ in sorted_outcomes[:2]}
assert len(expected_peaks.intersection(top_two_states)) >= 1, f"At least one expected peak should be in top 2 outcomes, got {top_two_states}"
```

```python
# The periodic input state |+0-> after QFT should concentrate on specific frequency peaks
# QFT of the periodic pattern should show peaks at states 000 and 100 (binary)
# which correspond to the fundamental frequency components

total_peak_probability = counts.get('000', 0) + counts.get('100', 0)
assert total_peak_probability > 0.7, f"Expected frequency peaks should have >70% probability, got {total_peak_probability:.3f}"

# Verify that the two main peaks are roughly equal (within 30% of each other)
prob_000 = counts.get('000', 0)
prob_100 = counts.get('100', 0)
if prob_000 > 0 and prob_100 > 0:
    ratio = min(prob_000, prob_100) / max(prob_000, prob_100)
    assert ratio > 0.7, f"Main frequency peaks should be roughly equal, ratio: {ratio:.3f}"

# Verify other states have significantly lower probabilities
other_states = ['001', '010', '011', '101', '110', '111']
max_other_prob = max(counts.get(state, 0) for state in other_states)
assert max_other_prob < 0.2, f"Non-peak states should have <20% probability, max found: {max_other_prob:.3f}"
```

```python
# The periodic input |+0-> = (|000> + |100> - |010> - |110>)/2
# After QFT, this should concentrate on specific frequency peaks
# Expected dominant outcomes should be '000' and '100' based on the periodicity

total_counts = sum(counts.values())
dominant_outcomes = ['000', '100']

# Check that the two expected dominant outcomes account for most measurements
dominant_count = sum(counts.get(outcome, 0) for outcome in dominant_outcomes)
dominant_fraction = dominant_count / total_counts

# Assert that at least 60% of measurements are in the expected frequency peaks
assert dominant_fraction >= 0.6, f"Expected frequency peaks only account for {dominant_fraction:.3f} of measurements"

# Verify that each expected peak has reasonable probability (at least 15% each)
for outcome in dominant_outcomes:
    outcome_fraction = counts.get(outcome, 0) / total_counts
    assert outcome_fraction >= 0.15, f"Outcome {outcome} has fraction {outcome_fraction:.3f}, below expected threshold"
```

```python
# The QFT on the periodic input |+0-> should concentrate probability
# on specific frequency components, not spread uniformly
total_measured = sum(counts.values())
assert total_measured == shots

# Check that the distribution is not uniform - QFT should create peaks
# For 3 qubits, uniform would be ~12.5% per outcome
uniform_probability = 1.0 / 8
max_outcome_fraction = max(counts.values()) / shots

# Assert that at least one outcome has significantly higher probability than uniform
assert max_outcome_fraction > 2 * uniform_probability

# Check that the distribution concentrates on a small number of outcomes
# Count how many outcomes have significant probability (>10% of total)
significant_outcomes = sum(1 for count in counts.values() if count / shots > 0.1)
assert significant_outcomes <= 4  # Should concentrate, not spread across all 8 possible outcomes

# Verify that the top outcomes contain most of the probability mass
sorted_counts = sorted(counts.values(), reverse=True)
top_3_probability = sum(sorted_counts[:3]) / shots
assert top_3_probability > 0.7  # Top 3 outcomes should capture >70% of probability
```

```python
# Expected peaks should be at states where the frequency component aligns with the periodicity

peak_count = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in peak_states)
peak_probability = peak_count / total_measured

# At least 80% of measurements should be on the expected frequency peaks

for state in non_peak_states:
    state_probability = counts.get(state, 0) / total_measured
```

```python
# The QFT of the periodic input |+0-> should concentrate on specific frequency peaks
# Expected peaks are at binary states 000 and 100 (decimal 0 and 4)
expected_peaks = {'000', '100'}

# Calculate total probability mass on expected peaks
peak_probability = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in expected_peaks)

# Calculate total probability mass on non-peak states
other_states = set(counts.keys()) - expected_peaks
non_peak_probability = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in other_states)

# Verify that most probability mass is concentrated on the expected peaks
assert peak_probability > 0.7, f"Expected peaks should have >70% probability, got {peak_probability:.3f}"

# Verify that non-peak states have significantly less probability
assert non_peak_probability < 0.4, f"Non-peak states should have <40% probability, got {non_peak_probability:.3f}"

# Verify that the two main peaks have reasonable individual probabilities
for peak in expected_peaks:
    if peak in counts:
        assert counts[peak] > 0.2, f"Peak {peak} should have >20% probability, got {counts[peak]:.3f}"
```

```python
# The periodic input state |+0-> creates frequency peaks at states 0 and 4 after QFT
# These correspond to the periodic structure with period 4 in the 3-qubit space
expected_peaks = {'000', '100'}  # states 0 and 4 in binary

# Calculate total probability mass on expected peaks
peak_probability = sum(counts.get(state, 0) for state in expected_peaks)

# Assert that most of the probability (>70%) concentrates on the expected peaks
assert peak_probability > 0.7, f"Expected peaks should contain >70% probability, got {peak_probability:.3f}"

# Assert that each expected peak has significant probability (>20%)
for peak in expected_peaks:
    prob = counts.get(peak, 0)
    assert prob > 0.2, f"Peak {peak} should have >20% probability, got {prob:.3f}"

# Assert that no unexpected state has more probability than the weakest expected peak
min_peak_prob = min(counts.get(peak, 0) for peak in expected_peaks)
for state, prob in counts.items():
    if state not in expected_peaks:
        assert prob <= min_peak_prob * 1.1, f"Unexpected state {state} has too high probability {prob:.3f}"
```
